headlines.title: Belgium fans erupt after latest-ever goal seals place in last 16
headlines.snippet: Belgium fans celebrate a dramatic 3-2 victory over Senegal in extra time.

headlines.title: Oil prices fall to levels not seen since start of US-Israel war on Iran
headlines.snippet: Brent falls below $71 a barrel amid reports of progress in talks to end the war.

headlines.title: Survivors look to foreign aid after the earthquakes in Venezuela
headlines.snippet: Al Jazeera’s Teresa Bo reports from a golf course turned relief centre in Venezuela.

headlines.title: California gets Bruce Lee Day in a first for US state’s Chinese Americans
headlines.snippet: Bruce Lee Day aims to honour the San Francisco-born martial arts legend as a cultural bridge and Asian-American icon.

headlines.title: US designates Ecuador’s Chone Killers gang as ‘terrorist’ organisation
headlines.snippet: Ecuador's government praises the US for backing President Noboa's campaign to dismantle gang-crime networks.

headlines.title: Pro-Palestine immigrant ousts 30-year veteran in Colorado Democratic primary
headlines.snippet: Pro-Palestine immigrant ousts 30-year veteran in Colorado Democratic primary Democratic Socialists in the US netted a landmark win on Tuesday after a 29-year-old Ethiopian immigrant and political newcomer unseated a member of Congress who has held that job since before she was born.

headlines.title: Tanker tracker says cargo ship in Hormuz has been stuck since March
headlines.snippet: Tanker tracker says cargo ship in Hormuz has been stuck since March A maritime traffic monitor says a foreign cargo ship that Iranian state media reported had run aground while using a "US-suggested route" in the Strait of Hormuz had "actually been stuck in this same spot since March".

headlines.title: Hormuz traffic holds steady but remains below pre-war levels
headlines.snippet: Maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz has remained steady but below pre-war levels, with 34 verified crossings recorded on Tuesday. This follows a recent drop, particularly after June 24th, when 70 transits were the highest since the US-Israeli war on Iran began.

headlines.title: Iranian deputy foreign minister says delegation raised US violations of MoU
headlines.snippet: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi stated that the Iranian delegation raised U.S. violations of Article 1 of a memorandum of understanding during meetings with Qatari and Pakistani mediators. The MoU reportedly calls for an end to war.

headlines.title: Iranian deputy foreign minister says frozen funds to be used for needed goods
headlines.snippet: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi announced that part of Iran's frozen assets in Qatar will be used to purchase necessary goods for the country. This comes after talks in Doha regarding the implementation of an agreement that halted war between Iran and the US.

headlines.title: Lebanon, Syria join CENTCOM-led Middle East security dialogue for first time
headlines.snippet: Lebanon and Syria have participated in a CENTCOM-led Middle East security dialogue for the first time. This marks a significant development in regional security cooperation.

headlines.title: World Bank approves Morocco clean energy project
headlines.snippet: The World Bank has approved $265 million in financing for Morocco to support the construction of a new pumped hydropower storage plant.

headlines.title: Trump sees progress as US, Iran hold Qatar talks
headlines.snippet: Trump indicated that indirect talks with Iran in Doha were progressing, suggesting that diplomacy was continuing despite recent exchanges of fire. This offers a tentative sign of diplomatic movement.

headlines.title: Attackers target homes of Greek ruling party figures, killing one
headlines.snippet: Attackers used homemade explosives targeting the homes of three politicians from Greece's ruling party on Wednesday, resulting in one fatality.

headlines.title: Turkey, Iraq discuss cooperation ahead of pipeline deal expiry
headlines.snippet: Turkey's energy minister met with Iraqi officials to discuss cooperation ahead of the expiration of the Iraq-Turkey Crude Oil Pipeline deal.

headlines.title: Body tasked with Gaza governance says Cyprus talks 'highly productive'
headlines.snippet: A committee responsible for Gaza's governance reported that talks held in Cyprus over two days were 'highly productive.' The discussions focused on alleviating suffering and reconstruction efforts.

headlines.title: نشرة الثالثة فجراً
Iran prevents transit of over 3,600 kilograms of methamphetamine to Europe
headlines.snippet: A senior Iranian anti-narcotics official announced the seizure of 3,660 kilograms of methamphetamine intended for Europe. Iran highlighted the cost it incurs for global security while facing sanctions in its drug interdiction efforts.

headlines.title: Iran warns UN of Israel’s ‘deliberate’ and ‘public’ threat against Leader of Islamic Revolution
headlines.snippet: Iran's UN Ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani warned the UN and Security Council about Israel's explicit threats against the Leader of the Islamic Revolution. He stated these threats were neither isolated nor rhetorical.

headlines.title: Pezeshkian: Government would have fully complied if Leader had ordered not to negotiate
headlines.snippet: President Masoud Pezeshkian stated that the government fully adhered to the guidance of the Leader of the Islamic Revolution. He affirmed that if the Supreme Leader had ordered against negotiations, no talks would have occurred.

headlines.title: Gharibabadi: Doha talks focused on US violations of MoU by US, frozen asstes
headlines.snippet: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi stated that talks in Doha focused on US violations of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), particularly concerning the ceasefire in Lebanon. A working group was formed to monitor the agreement's implementation.

headlines.title: Armed forces on high alert as Iran prepares for Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral
headlines.snippet: Iran's armed forces have increased their presence along the country's borders to ensure security during funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei. An army spokesperson confirmed the expanded deployment.

headlines.snippet: A royal committee described Israeli settlement expansion as a 'tool of colonial cleansing, elimination'. This statement highlights concerns about the expansionist policies.

headlines.snippet: Syria's president has named the final members required to finalize the country's first post-Assad parliament. This marks a step in the country's political transition.

headlines.snippet: Salam stated that they will not yield to blackmail or threats of civil war. This declaration addresses the political situation in Lebanon.

headlines.snippet: Lebanon has announced its intention to abolish the death penalty while in Paris. This move signifies a commitment to human rights reforms.

headlines.title: White House lifts export control on Anthropic that froze its most advanced models
headlines.snippet: The US government has lifted export controls on Anthropic's most advanced AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. The company announced that access will be restored soon.

headlines.title: Oman has delivered a proposal on the future of the Strait of Hormuz to the US, sources say
headlines.snippet: Oman has presented a proposal to the US and allies concerning the future of the Strait of Hormuz. The plan reportedly outlines service fees for shipping companies using the waterway.

headlines.title: The Strait of Hormuz pain has been China’s gain, The Asia Group says
headlines.snippet: The strategic advisory firm The Asia Group reported that China has benefited most from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The firm noted uneven economic impacts on Asian economies reliant on imports.

headlines.title: US, Iranian officials hold indirect, lower-level talks in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: US and Iranian officials are engaged in indirect, lower-level technical talks in Doha, facilitated by Qatari and Pakistani mediators. US special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met with Qatar's prime minister.

headlines.title: Egypt’s Former Irrigation Minister warns not to underestimate impact of GERD
headlines.snippet: Former Egyptian Minister of Water Resources and Irrigation, Mohamed Nasr-Eddin Allam, warned against underestimating the repercussions of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) on Egypt and Sudan.
